class.name: OpenXRSpatialAnchorCapability
class.brief_description: Implementation for handling spatial entity anchor logic.
class.description: This is an internal class that handles the OpenXR anchor spatial entity extension.

method.name: create_new_anchor
method.description: Creates a new anchor that will be tracked by the XR runtime. The [param transform] should be a transform in the local space of your [XROrigin3D] node. If [param spatial_context] is not specified the default will be used, this requires [member ProjectSettings.xr/openxr/extensions/spatial_entity/enable_builtin_anchor_detection] to be set. The returned tracker will track the location in case our reference space changes.

method.name: create_persistence_context
method.description: Create a new persistence context for storing persistent data. [b]Note:[/b] This is an asynchronous method and returns a [OpenXRFutureResult] object with which to track the status, discarding this object will not cancel the creation process. On success [param user_callback] will be called if specified. The result value for this function is the [RID] for our persistence context.

method.name: free_persistence_context
method.description: Free a persistence context previously created with [method create_persistence_context].

method.name: get_persistence_context_handle
method.description: Returns the internal handle for this persistence context. [b]Note:[/b] for GDExtension implementations.

method.name: is_persistence_scope_supported
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this persistence scope is supported by our spatial anchor capability. [b]Note:[/b] only valid after an OpenXR instance has been created.

method.name: is_spatial_anchor_supported
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if spatial anchors is supported by the hardware. Only returns a valid value after OpenXR is initialized.

method.name: is_spatial_persistence_supported
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if persistent spatial anchors is supported by the hardware. Only returns a valid value after OpenXR is initialized.

method.name: make_anchor_persistent
method.description: Changes this anchor to a persistent anchor. This means its location will be stored on the device and the anchor will be restored the next time your application starts. If [param persistence_context] is not specified the default will be used, this requires [member ProjectSettings.xr/openxr/extensions/spatial_entity/enable_builtin_anchor_detection] to be set. [b]Note:[/b] This is an asynchronous method. This method returns false if we fail to start the method. On success [param callback] will be called. On failure [signal anchor_persistent_failed] will be emitted.

method.name: make_anchor_unpersistent
method.description: Remotes the persistent data from this anchor. The runtime will not recreate the anchor when your application restarts. If [param persistence_context] is not specified the default will be used, this requires [member ProjectSettings.xr/openxr/extensions/spatial_entity/enabled] to be set. [b]Note:[/b] This is an asynchronous method. This method returns false if we fail to start the method. On success [param callback] will be called. On failure [signal anchor_unpersistent_failed] will be emitted.

method.name: remove_anchor
method.description: Remove an anchor previously created with [method create_new_anchor]. If this anchor was persistent you must first call [method make_anchor_unpersistent] and await its callback.

signal.name: anchor_persistent_failed
signal.description: Emitted when the asynchronous method [method make_anchor_persistent] fails to complete.

signal.name: anchor_unpersistent_failed
signal.description: Emitted when the asynchronous method [method make_anchor_unpersistent] fails to complete.

signal.name: persistence_creation_failed
signal.description: Emitted when persistence context creation failed.

constants.constant: Provides the application with read-only access (i.e. application cannot modify this scope) to spatial entities persisted and managed by the system. The application can use the UUID in the persistence component for this scope to correlate entities across spatial contexts and device reboots.

Persistence operations and data access is limited to spatial anchors, on the same device, for the same user and same app (using [method make_anchor_persistent] and [method make_anchor_unpersistent] functions)
